# WI-QUIC-CRT-0029 - QUIC CRT detached resumption-credential material capture work item

## Summary

Retain the minimum detached resumption-credential material on the managed client/runtime path and store the richer carrier as dormant internal state in a later client setup without widening the supported promise.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0131

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0029

## Planned Changes

- A richer immutable detached ticket carrier now retains ticket bytes, ticket nonce, ticket lifetime seconds, ticket age add, capture timestamp, and a resumption master secret derived from the supported handshake.
- The later managed client setup path stores that richer carrier as separate dormant internal state.
- The bridge-state ingress seam remains the transient source of ticket bytes, but the runtime keeps its own richer detached copy.
- Focused requirement-home tests cover richer export, absence before ingress, dormant import, duplicate-update non-replacement, server-role rejection, explicit early-data closure before and after handoff, unchanged handshake/bootstrap behavior, and the public no-hand-off/no-resumption/no-early-data guard.
- PSK derivation, 0-RTT packet processing, ClientHello replay, replay ownership, transfer, retry, key update, and any public API widening remain out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- public API widening
- ticket replay into ClientHello
- PSK derivation
- resumed handshake support
- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay behavior
- broad support for arbitrary post-handshake TLS messages
- fake replay or fake resumption success paths
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- key update
- broader session-management infrastructure
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the richer detached carrier or dormant import

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for richer detached carrier export, absence before capture, dormant import into later client setup, duplicate-update non-replacement, server-role rejection, explicit early-data closure before and after capture and handoff, the public no-hand-off/no-resumption/no-early-data guard, and unchanged handshake/bootstrap behavior with dormant state present; run the current CRT/API/INT guards and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; run repo-local SpecTrace validation and render checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Completion Notes

This is the landed narrow credential-capture slice. It retains opaque ticket bytes plus the minimum resumption-credential material required for a later honest resumption attempt, but it does not claim ClientHello reuse, PSK derivation, resumed-handshake support, or any public early-data promise.
